title: Sluissi
content:

The '''Sluissi''' were the indigenous [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]], located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. While their bodies above the waist were [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], their lower halves were serpentine, long and heavily muscled. Sluissi were noted for their calm temperament; aversion to rushing and impatience; compliance to protocol, no matter how trivial; and technical aptitude. Like many reptiles, Sluissi could use their forked tongues to sense heat.

Renowned for their ability to repair and maintain starships, the Sluissi were active in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] society almost since the beginning of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. This was because the Sluissi's technology was almost on par with other space-faring races such as [[Duro/Legends|Duros]] and [[Human/Legends|Humans]].

==Biology and appearance==
Sluissi's upper torsos were similar to a human's, with two arms, each with four-fingered hands.<ref name="UAA" /> One finger was an opposable digit. Their arms had almost winglike extensions of skin, which aided their movement while they were flat on the ground. The bottom half of their body was similar to a snake's, with a long, heavily muscled, singular tail.<ref name="EGAS" /> Their bodies were covered in fine scales which ranged in color from light brown to dark green. Other colored scales on Sluissi were not unheard of, but were rare. Sluissi had round black eyes and a swooping, hoodlike fold of tough skin at the back of their heads. They had forked tongues that aided their sense of smell.<ref name="UAA" /> The average member of the species weighed 60 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]].<ref name="TUR" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:Sluissi2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Sluissi]]
Sluissi were patient, sociable, relaxed, and easygoing beings,<ref name="UAA" /> famous for remaining calm under pressure. Their patient, calm nature—combined with their seeming inability to become genuinely upset or angry—often baffled, and sometimes even infuriated, other species.<ref name="DFRS">''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]''</ref> Extremely industrious, Sluissi loved to tinker with technology and mechanical objects,<ref name="UAA" /> and Sluissi technicians were able to improve the engine efficiency, power output and system response in even archaic, worn-out ships.<ref name="EGAS" />

The Sluissi were extremely methodical, and worked slowly. To them, starship construction was an art form. Although jobs done by Sluissi always took longer than expected, most clients were willing to be patient, because Sluissi technicians could make improvements upon or salvage vessels when no other mechanics could. Even the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] realized that rushing a Sluissi operation was a bad idea.<ref name="UAA" />

As well as [[Sluissese]], their native language, Sluissi spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], though they tended to slur and lisp many words, due to the snakelike structures of their tongues and mouths.<ref name="UAA" />

Common Sluissi names included Sirlahn Alsek, Vsil Ejahsa, Usahn L'sehl, Sekae N'sehnor and Mektiss Risohr.<ref name="UAA" />

==History==
The Sluissi joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] when it was still relatively young.<ref name="DFRS" /> When [[Duro/Legends|Duros]] and [[Human/Legends|Human]] scouts first discovered [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]], the Sluissi had only just begun to explore nearby systems with their own [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]] technology, and they eagerly embraced the concept of greater galactic union.<ref name="UAA" /> An advanced people, they established a number of major space yards throughout the [[Sluis sector/Legends|Sluis sector]], including the high-tech [[Sluis Van Central space station|Sluis Van space facility]],<ref name="DFRS" /> as well as on other colonies they had established to spur further interaction with offworld space travelers.<ref name="UAA" /> They ran efficient operations, where ship crews could find all the service, expertise and upgrades necessary after long journeys.<ref name="DFRS" /> The Sluissi quickly became known as one of the best starship maintenance species in the known galaxy.<ref name="UAA" />

[[File:VienneseCinnamon-EGAS.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A Sluissi]]
Shortly before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], they became disillusioned with the Republic, and were among the first to secede and join the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] cause.<ref name="Holonet">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|news/13328_4.html|Sluis Sector Secedes; Seswenna Restates Loyalty}}</ref> Nevertheless, some Sluissi were aligned with the Republic.<ref name="JT">''[[Jedi Trial]]''</ref>

After the Clone Wars, the Sluissi lived under Imperial occupation.<ref name="UAA" /> When this ended, they became valuable members of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] attacked three [[Planet/Legends|planets]] in the [[Sluis sector/Legends|Sluis sector]], in an attempt to steal warships from the Sluissi shipyards. The resulting [[Battle of Sluis Van]] almost ended in victory for the Empire. However, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] was in the area, captained by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and aided both the New Republic military and the Sluissi people.<ref name="DFRS" /><ref name="HTTE">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref> During the New Republic's rule, Mammon Hoole included an entry on the Sluissi in his publication ''[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''.<ref name="EGAS" />

==Sluissi in the galaxy==
Offworld Sluissi tended to work as tech specialists, engineers or mechanics, although it was not uncommon for a Sluissi to work as something else.<ref name="UAA" /> At least [[Unidentified Sluissi Jedi|one Sluissi]] was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and served as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. This individual was one of [[Lucien Draay]]'s [[Covenant Shadow]]s, a secret force of Jedi of whom all records had been erased to allow them to work undercover.<ref name="KOTOR 25" />

[[Ten Dorne]] was a Sluissi who worked on [[Shantipole Project]] with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]. The project revolved around the development of the [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]]. Shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Imperials captured Dorne. The experience made her doubt her devotion to the Rebel cause, so she decided to defect and later sold information about the Alliance to the Empire. She was later believed to be working at the [[Imperial Remnant]] shipyards on [[Vosteltig ship yards|Vosteltig]].<ref name="WBC">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']]</ref>

[[Sere Lure]] was a green-skinned female Sluissi engineer, who worked for [[Hydrospeare Corporation]]. Dr. Lure was a rarity; she was one of very few aliens permitted and willing to work in the development of Imperial technology.<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>

[[Candobar Inglet]] was the [[Khedive|ruler]] of Sluis Van and the Sluis sector during the Clone Wars and for several years beforehand. Just before the war, he and his sector seceded to the Confederacy of Independent Systems.<ref name="HN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|56|regional/13516_3.html|Sluis Van Offer Met with Doubt}}</ref>

Other Sluissi included [[Hass Sonax]], a female Sluissi slicer;<ref name="AJ15">{{JournalCite|15|The Great Herdship Heist}}</ref><ref name="Fair Prey">{{GamerCite|1|Fair Prey}}</ref> [[Corlissi Ludar]], the Sluissi senator who pledged Sluis Van's alliance with the CIS;<ref name="Holonet" /> [[Slith Skael]], the chief communications technician of the Intergalactic Communications Center towards the end of the Clone Wars;<ref name="JT" /> and [[Secles Uslopos]], a male Sluissi councilor to the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] crime lord [[Abdi-Badawzi]] on the planet [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]].<ref name="AJ2">{{JournalCite|2|Out of the Cradle}}</ref>

[[Alessi Quon]] was a male Sluissi scientist who worked for the [[Experi-Tech]] department of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 1">[[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 1|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 1]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
"[[« Statue » quo à Sluis Van]]," an issue of the French roleplaying-game magazine [[Casus Belli (magazine)|''Casus Belli'']], contains additional material on the Sluissi. According to this article, the Sluissi were a calmly methodical species who compensated for physical frailty with technological skill and ingenuity. In contrast to mentions of a [[Khedive]] in Clone Wars material, this source says that an elected parliament of a hundred members, known as the Council, governed Sluis Van, and throughout the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], their political consensus was that loyalty to the Empire had brought their world prosperity.

According to this material, the Sluissi joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in around [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]] thanks to a diplomatic mission led by [[Jonas Stern]] and [[Borsk Fey'lya]].

The novel ''[[Jedi Trial]]'' incorrectly depicts the Sluissi, Sluis Van, and the Sluis sector aligned with the Republic rather than the Confederacy.
